record_kind: HELD NODE — converged across a design conversation (continuation of the same session as the inspector/janitor/notifier and validations-migration work), deliberately NOT cast. Mutable by design: this grows as the thread does. Homeless on one axis Akien named out loud — 'I dunno if it's a librarian function or its own device' — so it is filed as a note, not yet typed.
owner: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC) gates writes. The design, the naming ('librarian', 'Hex'), and every advancing step are Akien's; CC's contribution is the framing (the two axes, the road convergence, the tenure reuse, the compilation trace).
the_core_loop:
THE KEYSTONE (Akien, 'very simple'): a query resolves by TRAVERSING THE GRAPH TREES (cheap, no inference). On a MISS — the graph cannot resolve it — the LLM is asked to provide THE NODES THAT LET IT RESOLVE, not the answer. The nodes are folded in; the graph re-resolves. The answer therefore ALWAYS comes from structure, never directly from inference, so re-asking the same question is a graph walk, not a second LLM call.

This IS Law 1 as a RUNTIME MECHANISM, not an analogy to it: 'the resolver is spent on the novel, not on re-deriving the settled; every answered question becomes structure.' The LLM (the expensive resolver) fires only on the novel (a graph miss); its output is structure (nodes); the settled is a traversal. Inference compilation (telos aim 1) and the shrinking-footprint aim are CONSEQUENCES of this loop, not separate goals. Self-limiting: within a domain in use, gaps fill, misses stop recurring, inference cost decays toward ~zero — novel domains cost inference, settled ones cost a graph walk.
embedding_is_the_path: Akien: 'the embedding IS the path thru the graph trees.' Consequence: EDGES ARE DERIVED, NOT STORED. You persist O(n) vectors and let proximity generate the paths at query time — the route between two stones is not recorded, it falls out of where they sit in the space. This is derive-don't-store landing on the correct side (CC's habitual reflex is the wrong side — see memory cc-reify-vs-flow), and it is WHY the graph trees belong in db_domain (vector similarity is the one thing a database does that git cannot) while everything articulated stays diffable in git. It also makes Cairn's own name literal: 'compiled navigation — stones stacked so the next mind doesn't re-derive the route.' The embedding is the compiled route between stones; the librarian produces the thing Cairn is named after.
two_axes:
The four activities Akien named (graph-tree learning from inference calls; find-patterns-in-a-clump; fact learning; de-instance-for-sharing) are not four devices — they are one operation at different coordinates on two axes.
  AXIS ONE — instance -> pattern. A fact is n=1 (a single citable truth); a pattern is n>=many (recurring structure across instances). Fact-learning is the BOTTOM of the same ladder pattern-finding climbs; 'find patterns in a clump' is: hand over a pile of instances, climb the ladder.
  AXIS TWO — storage physics. Continuous / similarity-structured -> db_domain (the graph trees). Discrete / articulated / diffable -> git, beside code (validations, intentions, the Laws). Orthogonal to axis one.
The librarian is the TRANSDUCER across axis two: it turns a dense region of the graph (db, tacit, statistical) into a distilled, nameable, why-carrying pattern (git, articulated, shareable). That single move is simultaneously 'find the pattern in the clump', 'extract a pattern to share', and 'de-instance for the exchange' — same transduction, different triggers.
the_same_road_as_the_roots_question: instance/fact -> pattern -> shared pattern is the SAME road as held-roots-as-stations-or-shelves's three stations (private particular -> de-instanced pattern -> gated identity). The librarian WALKS that road. Two structures derived on different days from different starts, cutting the same line — the same kind of convergence as the notifier and the inspector earlier today, and evidence toward STATIONS not shelves. CAVEAT that sharpens rather than closes the roots open: EXHAUST never gets on the road — logs and credentials in ~/.cairn are instances that will never become shareable patterns. So the roots may not be uniformly stations or shelves: knowledge flows through stations, exhaust just sits. A real refinement to held-roots-as-stations-or-shelves.
hex_and_the_corpus:
HEX IS CONCRETE NOW: the Apple M1 inference host — a physical machine that hosts local models, not an abstraction (this updates the earlier 'Hex + igors' shorthand). Because inference on owned M1 silicon costs electricity, not metered tokens, a large one-time corpus densification is INVESTMENT, not waste — it is compilation (pay embedding once, navigate forever without re-derivation; Law 1 / aim 1). CORPUS: a large body of ebooks, embedded on Hex, is the first LAYER-TWO seed under the two-db-layers ruling (held-pattern-exchange: layer one = schema + empty trees in git as a replayable recipe; layer two = a compiled dense snapshot, legitimately an artifact/dump). It is also the first real 'clump' for pattern-find — the librarian's opening job is standing.

This also corrects a caution CC raised one turn earlier: 'self-training must ride only the already-flowing inference stream.' That holds for REMOTE/paid inference; local Hex relaxes it — generating embedding calls to densify a corpus on owned silicon is fine.
graft_candidate_uu_embeddings_generator: UU (the prior iteration) had a GRAPH-TREE-BASED EMBEDDINGS GENERATOR that SELF-TRAINED VIA API CALLS, used for LIKE-type (similarity) database calls. It is a concrete graft candidate for the librarian's engine. The graft discipline is isolation.py's precedent: the MECHANISM crosses nearly intact (embedding generation, the self-train loop, the similarity/LIKE query — infra truth Cairn would not improve by rewriting), the DESIGN does not (ownership, write-gating, the charter, and critically the de-instancing chokepoint that strips particulars before publish — Cairn re-authors these). By Law 8 it grafts ONE TICKET AT A TIME with its own proof a hollow build couldn't pass, not pasted. SOURCE LOCATION: to be located in the UU tree so the future ticket has code to graft from.
device_or_function: RESOLVED 2026-07-22 (Akien's delegation framing). It is TWO things, cleanly: the LIBRARIAN is the OWNER of the commons graph; the EMBEDDINGS GENERATOR is a shared SERVICE that writes and reads owners' trees BY DELEGATION. Not a new rule — Law 6 verbatim ('delegated access happens only through the owner's gate'). This is why 'everybody grants the generator access' works without anyone surrendering ownership. (The earlier lean — 'don't split generation from graph yet' — is superseded: the split is ownership vs delegated-actor, which is exactly the clean seam, not a premature reification.)
ownership_delegation_and_asymmetric_inde:
THE OWNERSHIP MODEL (Akien, 2026-07-22):
  - The librarian OWNS the graph-tree data (Law 6). The embeddings generator PUTS and READS it by DELEGATION — it holds no ownership, it acts through the owner's gate.
  - ASYMMETRIC INDEXING -> DOMAIN SPECIALIZATION: trees from OTHER owners may INDEX the librarian's nodes (read/link INTO the commons), but NOT the reverse unless granted. So specialization layers ON TOP of the shared base: a specialist builds a tree that indexes the general commons and adds its own nodes, without the commons absorbing the specialist's noise. Private-DOWN by default.
  - This is the DEFAULT HALF of the privacy model; the pattern-exchange's DE-INSTANCING CHOKEPOINT is the OTHER half — deliberate grant-UP. Together: private by default, shared by explicit act, the same Law-6 gate on both.
  - ONE SHARED GENERATOR: 'a smart thing for everybody to grant access to unless they wanna do their own — which I can't imagine for the foreseeable future.' An owned n=1 assumption WITH a stated horizon (honest, not hidden). Build for one generator; do not PRECLUDE many.
  - IT PULLS A FILED db_domain EDGE: db_domain today gates by EXACT owner; its charter files 'delegated access ... later capabilities' as unbuilt. The librarian's delegation is the consumer that finally pulls that edge. Not a collision — a filed IOU that now has its puller, and the concrete next engineering dependency.
  - SMALL OPEN: when a query gap-fills, WHERE do the new nodes land? Natural answer — the querier's OWN tree (they own the query, the gap is theirs), promoted to the commons only by grant. Keeps fills private-by-default, consistent with the asymmetry. Not yet ruled.
self_validating_no_consumer_gap: Akien: 'once the nodes are deposited the query is run again to return the reply from the trees (self validating). No gap from the perspective of the consumer.' TRUE at the RESOLVABILITY level — the re-run confirms a path now exists, and the consumer never sees the miss (the gap-fill is transparent; a cold miss is just slower). The one thing 'self-validating' cannot reach is TRUTH: a COMPLETED path can still be a WRONG path. That is the provided-nodes-are-hypotheses-earn-tenure edge (see the_one_edge_provided_nodes_are_hypotheses). Not a hole — a filed edge.
the_one_edge_provided_nodes_are_hypothes: The loop is SELF-CHECKING — the LLM cannot quietly hallucinate, because its output is judged mechanically by whether the graph NOW RESOLVES (no completed path, no acceptance). But a COMPLETED path is not a TRUE path: the LLM could bridge a gap with plausible-but-wrong nodes and route to a wrong answer. So provided nodes are HYPOTHESES (Law 3, nothing known until measured). The mechanical gate is necessary, not sufficient. Sufficiency comes from TENURE: nodes that keep producing good resolutions survive; ones that don't are pruned — the SAME yield/tenure mechanism as the probe corpus (questions/). No new validation to invent; the librarian's nodes earn their keep the way probes do.
a_quiet_law_7_win: Because the answer comes from the graph and not from the LLM, a resolution is INSPECTABLE — you can see which nodes formed the path, unlike a raw LLM answer you must trust. The route is legible, which is what a record of truth wants (Law 7).
relation_to_the_spine: NOT on the spine (ground loop -> rack -> inference domain -> web server, MAP.md:888) — but the spine FEEDS it. inference_domain (the sole path to the host) is the prerequisite that makes the librarian SUBSCRIBABLE: the librarian learns from the inference stream by subscribing to that one gate (the notifier pattern settled this morning — gate posts the fact, subscriber evaluates its own predicate; event, not poll). And the librarian is db_domain's FIRST real durable tenant — it fills the empty room CC flagged right after the validations migration (db_domain now holds only the registry). The librarian was never a detour off the spine; it is what the spine's inference step pays out into.
traces_to: Law 1 — the resolve-or-generate loop IS 'the resolver spent on the novel, not re-deriving the settled', made a runtime mechanism
telos aim 1 (demonstrate inference compilation) — the loop compiles inference into graph structure; the embedding is the compiled route

telos aim 4 (build something that thinks the way Akien does) — you don't clone the intuition, you extract the patterns it runs on; the eight Laws + CP1-CP6 ARE distilled patterns (top of axis one, git side of axis two), and every /sorted that casts a design conversation is one distillation by hand — we have been running the librarian manually all along
telos aim 5 (self-improving) — the graph densifies against use; the system gets cheaper and better in the domains it works in
telos aim 3 (share tools with others) — the pattern-exchange consumes the librarian's git-side output (the distilled, de-instanced pattern); the librarian is the exchange's producer
Law 6 — the generation/graph ownership seam (filed, not yet named)
Law 3 — provided nodes are hypotheses until tenure measures them
Law 7 — a graph-sourced resolution is inspectable (the path is legible)
memory shrinking-footprint-event-not-poll — the loop is the mechanism that makes the footprint shrink asymptotically
completeness_read_2026-07-22:
Ran the /sorted completeness gate informally when Akien asked 'how does it look — almost sorted?'. The CONCEPT is settled and coheres unusually well (multiple independent threads from the same day converge on it: Law 1 as a runtime loop, the notifier/gate-subscription, the roots road, the two-db-layers, Law 6 delegation). 'Sorted design' is NOT yet 'cast node', and the gap is small and mostly DOWNSTREAM/spine-gated, not conceptual:
  - ASSUMPTION: the librarian's nodes are broadly-granted-readable by default (that is what makes it the commons); a specialization is any narrowly-granted tree. Made explicit here.
  - MISSING (for sharing, not for the core loop): the de-instancing chokepoint — filed in held-pattern-exchange, downstream.
  - FALSIFIABLE: candidates stated (see candidate_falsifier); state one at cast.
  - COLLIDES: no. It is Law 6 textbook and PULLS db_domain's filed delegated-access edge (a dependency, not a conflict).
  - CHILDREN ARE SPINE-GATED: subscription needs inference_domain (sole path); delegation needs db_domain's delegated-access edge. Both ahead of the librarian on the deliberately-unmoved spine.
RECOMMENDATION (CC): HOLD as the design node it is. Casting now would bind a gate-set to children that cannot move until the spine reaches them — buying tracking the held node already gives. Cast when the spine reaches inference_domain and db_domain grows delegated access.
not_cast_because: Still actively generating in conversation, AND gated on spine work deliberately placed ahead of it (inference_domain for subscription, db_domain delegated-access for the delegation). Casting now would freeze a shape mid-derivation (the premature-cast defect /sorted's gate exists to stop) and bind gates to children that cannot move. Held and mutable — the right resting state until the spine reaches it. Ripe to cast when: the falsifier is stated (candidates below) and its spine dependencies land.
candidate_falsifier: The hard test that separates a real librarian from a clustering script: could it distill a pattern Akien would recognize as one of his own — could it have found a Law? A weaker, sooner horizon: on the ebook corpus, does the resolve-or-generate loop's inference rate DECAY as the same domain is queried repeatedly (Law 1 holding), rather than staying flat (the graph not actually caching)?
provenance: Emerged 2026-07-22 immediately after the validations migration, when Akien named db_domain's anticipated clients: graph-tree learning from inference calls, owned by a 'librarian'. Then, in order: find-patterns-in-a-clump -> fact-learning ('also kind of pattern extraction') -> the UU embeddings-generator graft -> Hex is the M1 + the ebook corpus + 'the embedding IS the path' -> the resolve-or-generate core loop ('very simple'). Every advancing step was Akien's.
